fields.description:
## Why
R1's asserted-capability probe (plan-review) needs Pass-2 verification support: a dedicated `asserted_capability_confirmed` sub-answer lets the verifier refute a capability claim on the evidence instead of overloading `no_viable_alternative_explanation`. The kernel vocabulary is SHARED with code-review (`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py:GRADED_BINARY`), so this is the epic's highest-blast-radius change and sits behind the strictest gates (E5 byte-identical replay + E6 self-consistency).

## What
Add `asserted_capability_confirmed` to the shared kernel `GRADED_BINARY` (decide.py:31) as an **na-DEFAULT** graded sub-answer (registered in `verify_models._BINARY_NA_DEFAULT`), so it ABSTAINS from `validity()` (decide.py:56, excluded from the graded mean) everywhere the verifier does not answer it. The R5 verifier answers it non-`na` ONLY for the G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability cohort; `na` elsewhere. Because `_build_binary` (verify_models.py) is shared, BOTH the plan-review and code-review Binary models inherit it. Landed directly in the packaged kernel (no config flag), on E5 + E6 pass.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` — one entry in `GRADED_BINARY`.
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify_models.py` — `_BINARY_DESC` + `_BINARY_NA_DEFAULT` entry. (NEW module: the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from `verify.py` along the existing call-graph seam to hold the LOCKED 800-LOC hard cap — `verify.py` sat at exactly 800; names re-exported from `verify`, no import site changed.)
- `tests/unit/test_r5_asserted_capability.py` — invariance + registration tests.
- `docs/review-kernel.md` — na-default sub-answer + no-bump note.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gate-row evidence committed before landing: E5 byte-identical replay artifact (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json` — empty decision/priority/validity diff over the 161-row code-review corpus, `na` outside G6/E4/T3) AND E6 Exp A κ≥0.6 (recorded κ=0.874 PASS).
- [ ] `asserted_capability_confirmed` added to the shared `GRADED_BINARY` as an na-default graded sub-answer; both plan-review and code-review Binary models default it to `na`. Pinned by `tests/unit/test_r5_asserted_capability.py`.
- [ ] Code-review behavior pinned UNCHANGED by a regression test (the invariance property in `test_r5_asserted_capability.py` + the E5 replay driver): validity/decision byte-identical whether the field is absent or `na`.
- [ ] IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped — grounded correction of the stub AC: the field is a *validity* axis touching neither `impact_code` nor `impact_plan` SHAPE (`code_review/sidecar.py:34` bumps only on impact_code shape change), and the byte-identical property (E5) requires sidecars stay un-segmented. Rationale recorded in `docs/review-kernel.md` + the E5 writeup.
- [ ] Landed directly in the packaged kernel (no config flag) through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1); module-size hard-800 respected; `docs/review-kernel.md` updated.
